title: React Native & Authentication
description: Advanced patterns for mobile development and secure authentication

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of React Context API?
options: To share state across components without prop drilling
questions.answer: To share state across components without prop drilling
questions.explanation: Context API allows you to pass values through the component tree without having to pass props down manually at every level.

questions.question: When should you store authentication tokens?
options: In localStorage or secure storage (AsyncStorage in React Native)
questions.answer: In localStorage or secure storage (AsyncStorage in React Native)
questions.explanation: Tokens should be persisted securely so users stay logged in after app restart. Use AsyncStorage in React Native or secure storage for sensitive data.

questions.question: What does the useContext hook do?
options: Consumes a context value created by createContext
questions.answer: Consumes a context value created by createContext
questions.explanation: useContext allows functional components to subscribe to Context without needing a Consumer component.

questions.question: Why is it bad practice to store tokens in state only?
options: Tokens are lost on app refresh/navigation
questions.answer: Tokens are lost on app refresh/navigation
questions.explanation: Component state is destroyed when the app refreshes. Tokens must be persisted in storage (AsyncStorage, SecureStorage) to maintain sessions.

questions.question: What is the correct way to handle navigation state with authentication?
options: Switch screens based on token state in navigation
questions.answer: Switch screens based on token state in navigation
questions.explanation: Conditional rendering based on auth state (isLoggedIn) determines which stack navigator to show: Auth vs App stacks.

questions.question: What should you do with sensitive data returned from your PHP backend?
options: Store securely (tokens in SecureStorage, user data encrypted)
questions.answer: Store securely (tokens in SecureStorage, user data encrypted)
questions.explanation: Authentication tokens and sensitive user data should never be logged or exposed. Use secure storage mechanisms provided by React Native.

questions.question: What pattern prevents unnecessary re-renders in Context consumers?
options: Split contexts by domain (Auth, Data, UI)
questions.answer: Split contexts by domain (Auth, Data, UI)
questions.explanation: Splitting contexts prevents all consumers from re-rendering when any value changes. Only components using changed values re-render.

questions.question: How should you handle API calls in React Native?
options: Make them directly in render functions
Use useEffect hooks with proper cleanup
Call them in button click handlers without state management
questions.answer: Use useEffect hooks with proper cleanup
questions.explanation: useEffect let's you manage side effects properly with dependency arrays and cleanup functions for subscriptions/cancellations.

questions.question: What should happen when a token expires?
options: Show an error message and force re-login
Silently refresh token; if fails, redirect to login
questions.answer: Silently refresh token; if fails, redirect to login
questions.explanation: Best practice: Attempt to refresh the token server-side. Only force login if refresh fails, minimizing user interruption.

questions.question: What's the best way to handle login errors?
options: Show user-friendly message + log for debugging
questions.answer: Show user-friendly message + log for debugging
questions.explanation: Users need clear feedback, but developers need detailed logs for debugging. Balance UX with debugging needs.

questions.question: What does StyleSheet.create() provide?
questions.explanation: StyleSheet.create() optimizes styles by assigning IDs and sending only the ID through bridge, not full objects repeatedly.

questions.question: In your project structure, what's the purpose of separating components, screens, and services?
options: Components=reusable UI; Screens=full pages; Services=API/business logic
questions.answer: Components=reusable UI; Screens=full pages; Services=API/business logic
questions.explanation: This separation follows best practices: reusable components, screen compositions, and centralized API logic for maintainability.
